title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
subtitle: The landmark exploration of how our minds make decisions — and why we're often unaware of the hidden forces shaping our judgments.
author: Daniel Kahneman
themes: The remembering self vs the experiencing self
awards: National Academy of Sciences Best Book Award (2012)
New York Times Ten Best Books of 2011
Globe and Mail Best Books of the Year (2011)
Presidential Medal of Freedom (2013, awarded to Kahneman)
excerpt: Your mind has two characters: one that jumps to conclusions with effortless confidence, and another that questions everything — but only when it has the energy. This book reveals the hidden war between them, and why understanding that conflict is the closest we can get to thinking clearly.
